statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Description
You are given two positive integers $ n $ ( $ 1 \le n \le 10^9 $ ) and $ k $ ( $ 1 \le k \le 100 $ ). Represent the number $ n $ as the sum of $ k $ positive integers of the same parity (have the same remainder when divided by $ 2 $ ).

In other words, find $ a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_k $ such that all $ a_i>0 $ , $ n = a_1 + a_2 + \ldots + a_k $ and either all $ a_i $ are even or all $ a_i $ are odd at the same time.

If such a representation does not exist, then report it.

Input Format
The first line contains an integer $ t $ ( $ 1 \le t \le 1000 $ ) — the number of test cases in the input. Next, $ t $ test cases are given, one per line.

Each test case is two positive integers $ n $ ( $ 1 \le n \le 10^9 $ ) and $ k $ ( $ 1 \le k \le 100 $ ).

Output Format
For each test case print:

- YES and the required values $ a_i $ , if the answer exists (if there are several answers, print any of them);
- NO if the answer does not exist.

The letters in the words YES and NO can be printed in any case.

Examples
Sample 1
Input
8
10 3
100 4
8 7
97 2
8 8
3 10
5 3
1000000000 9
Output
YES
4 2 4
YES
55 5 5 35
NO
NO
YES
1 1 1 1 1 1 1 1
NO
YES
3 1 1
YES
111111110 111111110 111111110 111111110 111111110 111111110 111111110 111111110 111111120

solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1352B - Same Parity Summands
Consider two cases: when we choose all odd numbers and all even numbers. In both cases let's try to maximize the maximum. So, if we choose odd numbers, let's try to take $$$k-1$$$ ones and the remainder $$$n-(k-1)$$$. But we need to sure that $$$n-k+1$$$ is greater than zero and odd. And in case of even numbers, let's try to take $$$k-1$$$ twos and the remainder $$$n-2(k-1)$$$. We also need to check that the remainder is greater than zero and even. If none of these cases is true, print "
NO
".
